T.value: Time to failure of a car, with constant failure rate (exponential distribution)
T.description: Random variable representing the time to failure of a new car, following an exponential distribution (constant failure rate).

guarantee_period.description: The guarantee period of 18 months (1.5 years), used both as the conditioning threshold (T ≥ 1.5) and to determine the failure rate from the return rate assumption.

max_return_rate.description: The assumed maximum proportion of new cars returned within the guarantee period, i.e., P(T ≤ 1.5) = 0.05, used to determine the failure rate λ.

time_horizon.description: The time horizon of 3 years, used in the event T ≤ 3 for computing the conditional probability of failure.
